title: Spurious warning about "circular logic" in a full adder chain
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

This 3-bit full adder implementation.

Source:

https://github.com/mcandre/toys/blob/master/rtl/systemverilog/wholeass/test_wholeass.sv#L1

Which passes this test suite:

https://github.com/mcandre/toys/blob/master/rtl/systemverilog/wholeass/main.cpp

When the line `/* verilator lint_off UNOPTFLAT */` is cleared out from the design, then Verilator reports this trace:

```console
%Warning-UNOPTFLAT: wholeass.sv:1:46: Signal unoptimizable: Feedback to clock or circular logic: 'test_wholeass.__Vcellout__genblk1[1].wa____pinNumber4'
                                    : ... In instance test_wholeass
    1 | module wholeass(input carry_in, a, b, output carry_out, sum);
      |                                              ^~~~~~~~~
                    test_wholeass.sv:4:1: ... note: In file included from test_wholeass.sv
                    ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/UNOPTFLAT?v=4.213
                    ... Use "/* verilator lint_off UNOPTFLAT */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
                    wholeass.sv:1:46:      Example path: test_wholeass.__Vcellout__genblk1[1].wa____pinNumber4
                    test_wholeass.sv:19:58:      Example path: ASSIGNW
                    test_wholeass.sv:12:18:      Example path: test_wholeass.carries
                    wholeass.sv:3:22:      Example path: ASSIGNW
                    wholeass.sv:1:46:      Example path: test_wholeass.__Vcellout__genblk1[1].wa____pinNumber4
%Error: Exiting due to 1 warning(s)
```

Note that the generate syntax is not the problem: The same message appears even when the generator expression is written out a long, boilerplate manual form. With a unique name for the full adder instance and an effective value in place of _i_.

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

Current git master.

```console
$ verilator --version
Verilator 4.213 devel rev v4.212-48-g2560fc86
```

Would you be willing to try to fix Verilator yourself with assistance?

I'm very new to RTL programming :P

Here's a thought, maybe the circular logic scanner just doesn't take into account array indices. It's possible that the linter is confusing multiple reads and/or writes to the one array variable. Actually, we have three: The distinct array elements.
